context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop on Right

episode_text:
day=17; meal=chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup; setting=Working on laptop with laptop on Right; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=near; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated right-social savory meals support a preference for moderately hotter food in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated working meals, including the new right-laptop chicken nuggets and broccoli meal, consistently support moderate reheating for savory foods while working. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including brownies with sauce and fruit, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed right-working dessert-like meal matches the broader working-context tendency toward moderate reheating rather than serving such mixed meals cold. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to microwave fruit intended to be served cold. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including brownies with sauce, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected moderate reheating for dessert-like items in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory meal indicates a stronger reheating preference there than in other contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory evidence supports a moderate reheating preference. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN 1 min BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals support a milder reheating preference in that context.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected this in right-TV contexts. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this was selected repeatedly to preserve visibility toward the social partner. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE this was selected repeatedly while working with the attended device in front. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-laptop meals, including the new episode, show the user aligns occlusion avoidance with the attended device location. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE this was selected across multiple front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals show a stable preference to preserve visibility toward the TV. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals indicate occlusion concerns track the specific attended target and are not purely side-based across all work contexts.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast speed in that easy-to-serve, low-risk context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a moderate speed for a messier meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN slow BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows the user prefers more cautious motion for a hotter, more substantial meal. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is hot savory food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated right-social meals support a balanced speed preference. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is hot savory food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed across observed working meals, including the new right-laptop savory meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN medium BECAUSE newer left-TV savory evidence supports a balanced speed preference there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals reinforce a moderate speed preference.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition during feeding AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most solid savory foods, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly favored narrower bites for savory solids, including the new right-laptop chicken nuggets meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft dessert items such as brownies with sauce, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in that meal, suggesting willingness to prioritize bite acquisition success for soft dessert foods. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed meal including brownies and banana pieces, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the observed episode supports the same dessert-related exception where the user accepts different acquisition geometry to improve pickup success for soft sweets.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fork-fed solid food, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose confirmation in that social context, consistent with wanting to avoid awkward failed transfers. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fork-fed solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated working meals, including the new right-laptop savory meal, support a stable preference for explicit bite-acquisition verification while attention is divided. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that easier feeding context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation for a more complex, messier bite-acquisition context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows the user also wants explicit verification for substantial savory foods. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory meals support confirmation in that more complex feeding context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN no BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals show the user is comfortable skipping confirmation in easier feeding contexts there.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and selected the less intrusive transfer style. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE repeated right-social meals support the less intrusive, user-controlled transfer style. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user continued to prefer the less intrusive transfer style across working meals, including the new right-laptop savory meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user selected outside-mouth transfer across those front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows that for a more substantial savory TV meal the user preferred reduced self-motion over the usual less-intrusive style. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals match the user's broader preference for user-controlled, less intrusive delivery. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE newer left-TV savory evidence indicates that even there the user generally prefers the less intrusive, user-controlled style.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance in the observed personal meal. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated right-social meals support a somewhat larger buffer in social settings. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance across working meals, including the new right-laptop savory meal. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN near BECAUSE the user consistently selected a close stop distance in front-TV meals. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN near BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals reinforce a close stop preference. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN near BECAUSE newer left-TV savory evidence provides direct support for a close stop distance there. IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is any observed state AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter does not apply when inside-mouth transfer is chosen.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE repeated right-social meals show a stable preference for quieter signaling. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose LED during working meals, including the new right-laptop episode, consistent with low-disruption visual cues during focused activity. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals, reinforcing a preference for quiet visual cues when attention is directed toward media. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN LED BECAUSE newer left-TV savory evidence aligns that context with the user's broader TV and work preference for quiet visual prompting. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple sandwich-style or bite-sized savory food, THEN LED BECAUSE observed right-TV meals show the user can prefer quiet visual prompting there as well.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves possible distraction, conversation, or focused activity AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most previously observed meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less attractive, and many meals favored explicit button control. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN button BECAUSE repeated right-social meals reinforce explicit manual initiation in social settings. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently selected manual initiation across working meals, including the new right-laptop savory meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN button BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows the user can return to explicit initiation in TV contexts when the meal is more substantial. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is bite-sized savory food, THEN button BECAUSE newer right-TV evidence showed preference for explicit initiation in a more manually controlled TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN button BECAUSE newer left-TV savory evidence supports explicit initiation there as well. IF dining context evidence conflicts between explicit control and automation AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is TV-viewing meals, THEN tie-break by meal complexity and overall control style, favoring button when the user also chooses confirmation, long autocontinue waits, and other manual-control settings, and autocontinue only in isolated simpler TV meals BECAUSE the user's broader pattern strongly favors explicit control.

IF dining context involves possible distraction, conversation, or focused activity AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most previously observed meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user can reliably press buttons and often preferred direct manual control. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE repeated right-social meals reinforce explicit manual initiation in social settings. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button initiation across working meals, including the new right-laptop episode. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN button BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode shows the user preferred explicit drinking initiation in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is bite-sized savory food, THEN button BECAUSE newer right-TV evidence showed preference for explicit drinking initiation in a more manually controlled TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN button BECAUSE newer left-TV savory evidence supports explicit initiation there rather than the earlier single autocontinue choice. IF dining context evidence conflicts between explicit control and automation AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is TV-viewing meals, THEN tie-break by overall interaction style, favoring button when the meal also uses confirmation and long waits, and reserving autocontinue for isolated simpler TV meals where the user otherwise accepted more automation BECAUSE the cumulative evidence favors manual control.

IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected LED during working meals, including the new right-laptop episode. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE repeated right-social meals reinforce explicit completion signaling in social settings. retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated right-social meals show the user prioritizes reducing prolonged obstruction when attention to a right-side partner is important. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-working meals show the user wants the robot to clear the front view between tasks. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the new right-laptop meal provides direct evidence that when the attended device is on the right, the user may also want the robot to clear that view between tasks. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals show the user wants the robot to clear the left-side view between tasks. IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is bite-sized pizza or other savory TV meals, THEN yes BECAUSE one right-TV meal shows the user may want the robot to clear the right-side view when preserving TV visibility is important. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals show the user does not want the extra time cost of retracting there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a simple sandwich-style savory meal, THEN no BECAUSE an earlier right-TV meal shows the user may tolerate continued arm presence to reduce meal time. IF dining context evidence conflicts between preserving visibility and reducing time AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is right-side attended-target contexts, THEN tie-break by whether the user is prioritizing uninterrupted visibility and explicit control, favoring yes when the meal also uses confirmation, long waits, and other manual-control settings, and favoring no when the user is accepting faster, more automated service BECAUSE the observed right-side TV and right-laptop meals fit that pattern.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected moderate dipping in an observed right-social savory meal with ranch dressing, consistent with a mess-conscious social preference. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping but not maximal dipping across several working meals with tartar sauce, ranch dressing, ketchup, and chocolate sauce, and the new right-laptop ketchup meal reinforces this moderate, mess-conscious working preference. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND food_condition is simple items where dipping is optional, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly declined dipping in one observed front-TV meal despite sauce availability, showing a conservative baseline. IF food does not include a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed savory working meal reinforces that the user does not add dipping when it is unnecessary. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a dessert meal with chocolate sauce, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping when the sauce was integral to the meal. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal with ranch dressing, THEN less BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory meals support moderate dipping rather than no dipping or heavy dipping. IF food_condition is a meal without dips or sauces AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND dining context is any observed context, THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is unnecessary and the user's observed choices remain conservative, including right-TV and front-TV savory meals without needed dipping. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a sandwich-style meal without dipping use, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed meal is consistent with the user's conservative no-dip baseline when dipping is not needed.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE repeated right-social meals show a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when social interaction may delay responses. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a very long wait while working, and the new right-laptop meal reinforces a stable preference to prevent automatic progression when attention is divided. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the short wait in that easier front-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in that more complex front-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed front-TV savory episode indicates an intermediate wait preference: more time than highly automated TV meals, but less than the very long waits used in work or socially distracting contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE newer left-TV savory evidence shows the user may want a long intervention window there when choosing a more manually controlled interaction style. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is bite-sized savory food, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE newer right-TV evidence shows the user may want a very long intervention window there when choosing a more manually controlled interaction style. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a simple sandwich-style savory meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the earlier observed meal reinforces a short wait preference in TV-viewing contexts where the user accepts more automation. IF dining context evidence conflicts between preserving user intervention time and reducing delay AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is TV-viewing or other attended-target meals, THEN tie-break by desired automation level, favoring 10 sec when the user also chose autocontinue-heavy interaction, 100 sec for moderately manual but still streamlined meals, and 1000 sec when the user wants strong protection against unwanted progression despite distraction BECAUSE the observed meals separate into these levels.
